frameworks.name: lossy data compression / chunking
frameworks.one_line: Similar features are jointly encoded to optimize limited working-memory capacity.

frameworks.name: center-surround dynamics
frameworks.one_line: Neural mechanism proposed to implement the chunking process.

frameworks.name: independent-item encoding models
frameworks.one_line: Standard VWM models assuming items are stored independently.

frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: Chunking policy is optimized via reinforcement signals.

core_question: Does visual working memory exploit chunking of similar features rather than encoding items independently?
key_assumptions: Working-memory capacity is limited and shared across items
Similar features can be jointly represented without separate slots
Recall precision and effective capacity trade off
manipulations: Feature similarity between memoranda in a canonical VWM task
Comparison of chunking vs independent-encoding model fits
key_findings: Performance advantages when similar features could be chunked
Precision detriments accompanied the capacity gain
Interitem dependencies and trial-to-trial adjustments diagnostic of chunking
Center-surround chunking model fit human data better than independent-item models
interpretations: Chunking is a rational lossy-compression strategy for VWM
Apparent individual capacity differences reflect differences in chunking implementation
